Question: What is the difference between an $$x \text{-}$$ intercept and a zero of a polynomial function $$f ?$$
Answer: The $$x \text{-}$$ intercept is where the graph of the function crosses the $$x \text{-}$$ axis, and the zero of the function is the input value for which $$f ( x ) = 0.$$

Question: If a polynomial function of degree $$n$$ has $$n$$ distinct zeros, what do you know about the graph of the function?
Explain how the Intermediate Value Theorem can assist us in finding a zero of a function.
Answer: If we evaluate the function at $$a$$ and at $$b$$ and the sign of the function value changes, then we know a zero exists between $$a$$ and $$b .$$

Question: Explain how the factored form of the polynomial helps us in graphing it.
If the graph of a polynomial just touches the $$x \text{-}$$ axis and then changes direction, what can we conclude about the factored form of the polynomial?
Answer: There will be a factor raised to an even power.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, graph the polynomial functions. Note $$x \text{-}$$ and $$y \text{-}$$ intercepts, multiplicity, and end behavior.
Question: $$g ( x ) = ( x + 4 ) ( x - 1 )^{2}$$
Answer: x -intercepts, $$( 1, 0 )$$ with multiplicity 2, $$( - 4, 0 )$$ with multiplicity 1, $$y \text{-}$$ intercept $$( 0, 4 )$$ . As $$x \vec{} - \infty$$ , $$g ( x ) \vec{} - \infty$$ , as $$x \vec{} \infty$$ , $$g ( x ) \vec{} \infty$$ .

Answer: x -intercepts $$( 3 , 0 )$$ with multiplicity 3, $$( 2 , 0 )$$ with multiplicity 2, $$y \text{-}$$ intercept $$( 0 , - 108 )$$ . As $$x \vec{} - \infty$$ , $$k ( x ) \vec{} - \infty$$ , as $$x \vec{} \infty$$ , $$k ( x ) \vec{} \infty .$$

Answer: x -intercepts $$( 0, 0 ) , ( - 2, 0 ) , ( 4, 0 )$$ with multiplicity 1, $$y$$ -intercept $$( 0, 0 ) .$$ As $$x \vec{} - \infty$$ , $$n ( x ) \vec{} \infty$$ , as $$x \vec{} \infty$$ , $$n ( x ) \vec{} - \infty .$$
